headline: United Rentals, Inc. First Quarter 2026 Conference Call and Audio Webcast Thursday, April 23, 2026 at 8:30 a.m. (ET)
summary: STAMFORD, Conn., April 08, 2026--United Rentals, Inc. (NYSE: URI) will hold its first quarter 2026 conference call with Matt Flannery, chief executive officer, and Ted Grace, chief financial officer, on Thursday, April 23, 2026 at 8:30 a.m. Eastern Time.

headline: Shares of companies within the broader industrial sector are trading higher amid overall market strength and easing energy costs due to the U.S.-Iran ceasefire agreement and President Trump's social media post that signaled a de-escalation of regional conflict and supply-chain disruption.
summary: Shares of companies within the broader industrial sector are trading higher amid overall market strength and easing energy costs due to the U.S.-Iran ceasefire agreement and President Trump's social media post that signaled a de-escalation of regional conflict and supply-chain disruption.

headline: Shares of industrial distribution companies are trading higher driven by easing energy costs after a U.S.-Iran ceasefire deal. Also, President Trump's social media post signaled a de-escalation of the Middle East conflict and supply chain concerns.
summary: Shares of industrial distribution companies are trading higher driven by easing energy costs after a U.S.-Iran ceasefire deal. Also, President Trump's social media post signaled a de-escalation of the Middle East conflict and supply chain concerns.

headline: WillScot Holdings (WSC) Is Up 5.1% After Rental Fleet Write-Offs And EBITDA Decline Has The Bull Case Changed?
summary: In recent days, WillScot Holdings reported a 13% year-over-year decline in EBITDA, a 13% drop in units on rent, and wrote off roughly 15% of its rental fleet, highlighting worsening financial and operational conditions. These fleet impairments, combined with rising leverage and intensifying competition from United Rentals, point to mounting pressure on WillScot’s core rental economics and future flexibility. Given the recent rental fleet write-offs, we’ll now examine how this development...
